basic_details.job_title: Income Tax Department Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 12 MTS, Steno and Tax Assistant Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Income Tax Department Odisha (Income Tax Department Odisha)
basic_details.post_name: Sports Quota - MTS, Steno and Tax Assistant
basic_details.short_information: This recruitment is open exclusively to meritorious sportspersons who meet the sports eligibility conditions described later in this article - it is not a general recruitment drive. Also read: Bank of Baroda Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for VP, DVP Posts

age_limit.age_details: Tax Assistant: 18 to 27 years, Stenographer Grade-II: 18 to 27 years, Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS): 18 to 25 years
age_limit.details: The age limit varies by post, and sportspersons get an additional relaxation on top of the standard limit. This section explains both the base age limit and the relaxation rules. | Tax Assistant: 18 to 27 years Stenographer Grade-II: 18 to 27 years Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS): 18 to 25 years | As per DoPT O.M. No. 15012/3/84-Estt.(D) dated 12-11-1987, sportspersons may be allowed a relaxation in the upper age limit of up to a maximum of 5 years (10 years for SC/ST candidates). This relaxation applies only if the candidate fulfils all other eligibility conditions and furnishes a certificate in the prescribed format as per DoPT O.M. No. 1720781414772 dated 04-03-2024.

qualification.details: This recruitment is reserved for candidates with a genuine, verifiable record of representing a state, country, university, or school in recognized sports competitions. Candidates without such achievements are not eligible, regardless of educational qualification. | Applicants must have represented a State or the country in a National or International competition; OR been a medal winner in Junior National Championships, Khelo India Youth Games (above 18 years), Khelo India Winter Games, Khelo India Para Games, Khelo India University Games, or School Games Federation of India (SGFI) events; OR represented their University in Inter-University Tournaments; OR represented a State School Team in National School Games - in any of the eligible sports listed below. | Also Read: Northeast Frontier Railway Scouts and Guides Quota Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 22 Posts | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

vacancy_details.Classification: General Central Service, Group 'C', Non-Gazetted, Ministerial
General Central Service, Group 'C', Non-Gazetted, Ministerial
General Central Service, Group 'C', Non-Gazetted, Non-Ministerial

selection_process: The selection process differs depending on the post applied for. Tax Assistant and Stenographer Grade-II go through two phases
while MTS selection is based on a single merit list.
eligible candidates are shortlisted based on their application details and sports achievements. For Tax Assistant
candidates equal to 4 times the number of vacancies (28 candidates) are shortlisted for Phase 2. For Stenographer Grade-II
10 candidates are shortlisted for Phase 2. In Phase 2
shortlisted candidates appear for a post-specific skill test at the Pr. CCIT Office
at their own expense. The final merit list combines performance from both phases.
Selection for MTS is based purely on the merit list prepared from sports achievement scores
with no separate skill test stage.
A waiting list will be maintained: 10 candidates for Tax Assistant
how_to_apply: There is no online application option for this recruitment - candidates must apply entirely offline by post. Here is the exact process to follow.
Download and fill the application form as per Annexure-II of the official notification, in block letters using blue/black ball point pen.
Mark your post preference clearly using '1', '2', '3' in order of preference, and 'X' for posts you are not applying for.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents listed above.
Place the completed application in a closed envelope, superscribed: "Application for appointment of meritorious sportsperson in Income Tax Department, Odisha Region- 2026."

Send the envelope by Registered Post or Speed Post only to: Deputy Commissioner of Income Tax (Hqrs.) (Admn. & Vig.), O/o the Pr. Chief Commissioner of Income Tax, 1st Floor, Aayakar Bhawan, Rajaswa Vihar, Bhubaneswar-751007.
Ensure the application reaches the office by 31-07-2026, 6:00 PM (10-08-2026 for candidates from North Eastern States, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Lakshadweep, and Jammu & Kashmir).
Only one application per candidate is allowed - submitting more than one will result in all applications being rejected. Applications received after the last date will not be considered.

_labels.click_here: PDF Download Link / Detailed Eligibility, Sports Disciplines & Application Format

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Income Tax Department Odisha Sports Quota Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The completed application must reach the office by 31-07-2026, 6:00 PM, by Registered/Speed Post. Candidates from North Eastern States, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Lakshadweep, and Jammu & Kashmir have until 10-08-2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 12 total vacancies - 7 Tax Assistant, 1 Stenographer Grade-II, and 4 Multi-Tasking Staff posts.

faq.question: Q3. Can a non-sportsperson apply for these posts?
faq.answer: No, this recruitment is exclusively for meritorious sportspersons who meet the sports eligibility conditions, such as representing a state, country, university, or school in recognized competitions.

faq.question: Q4. Is there an online application process?
faq.answer: No, applications must be submitted offline by Registered or Speed Post only. No other mode of application will be accepted.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification for Tax Assistant?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University, along with a data entry speed of 8,000 key depressions per hour.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for these posts?
faq.answer: The age limit as on 01-08-2026 is 18-27 years for Tax Assistant and Stenographer Grade-II, and 18-25 years for MTS, with age relaxation of up to 5 years (10 years for SC/ST) for sportspersons.

faq.question: Q7. How is the merit list prepared for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The merit list is based on a sports achievement scoring system, where points are awarded according to the level of competition (International, National, University, Khelo India, or School) and the position achieved, subject to qualifying the post-specific skill test where applicable.

faq.question: Q8. Is a skill test required for all posts?
faq.answer: A skill test is required for Tax Assistant (data entry speed) and Stenographer Grade-II (dictation and transcription). MTS selection is based purely on the sports achievement merit list, with no skill test.

faq.question: Q9. Up to which date are sports achievements considered?
faq.answer: Only sports achievements and participation up to 31-03-2026 are considered for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q10. Where can candidates find the official notification and detailed eligibility criteria?
faq.answer: The detailed eligibility criteria, list of sports disciplines, and application format are available on www.incometaxindia.gov.in.
